page: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Esv7 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes hyper-threading with 2 threads per core, a 480 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The system is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded performance. Single-threaded operations show strong results, with gzip compression reaching 32.55 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 13.6 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is average at 2034.29. However, multi-core and memory write benchmarks are weaker, with multi-core stress-ng scoring 4104.86 and large-block memory write bandwidth limited to 22.69 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the E4s_v7 offers a high memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The primary tradeoff lies in its compute density; with only 2 physical cores, multi-threaded scaling is limited, as evidenced by the weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and stress-ng scores. Additionally, the lack of local storage requires reliance on external premium storage options.

This server configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications with moderate parallel processing needs. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and single-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from the 32.0 GB RAM capacity and high single-threaded compression performance.
description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Esv7 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression at 32.55 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 13.6 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand heavy parallel processing. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-threaded performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-threaded performance.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high single-threaded performance and a generous memory-to-vCPU ratio for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
Strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression performance
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and caching applications
